name: Bronze Cube
description: A 10 cm × 10 cm × 10 cm solid bronze cube. Standard tin bronze alloy: 88% copper, 12% tin by mass. Volume = 10⁻³ m³. Density of bronze ≈ 8800 kg/m³. Mass = 8800 × 10⁻³ = 8.8 kg. Copper contributes 95.7% of the electron contribution (Z=29 vs Z=50 for Sn) but tin's heavier nucleus shifts the nuclear binding energy profile. A classic benchmarking solid — uniform composition, known geometry, no voids.
